name: the spontaneous frost forms
identity: Seasonal, hexagonal frost formations that emerge in the coldest hours of autumn, stable and persistent longer than natural ice. They exist as part of the unintended patterns within the Quiet Weave, enhancing the environment without causing harm.
current_status: Stable and persistent within the autumnal environment, forming as part of the larger patterned systems described in the Quiet Weave. Their presence is noted as a natural extension of the existing ecological and environmental systems.

appearance.value: seasonal, hexagonal, stable, persistent longer than natural ice

behavior.value: appear only in coldest hours of autumn, persist longer than natural ice

origin.value: unintended patterns within the Quiet Weave

volatile_state.location: autumnal environment, particularly in cold hours
volatile_state.current_activity: forming as part of the Quiet Weave's unintended patterns
